preview_text:
The model rates Germany as a slight favorite at 35%, with Ecuador at 33% and a draw at 32%. The gap is narrow: Germany holds only a 2-percentage-point edge, suggesting the teams are closely matched by the statistical forecast and that Ecuador has a credible path to victory or a stalemate.

The W/D/L spread reflects competitive balance. No outcome commands majority probability, and all three results fall within a compressed range of 31–35 percentage points. A draw emerges as plausible at nearly one-third, consistent with evenly-contested play. The model sees this as a genuinely open match.

The market—both sportsbooks and Polymarket—diverges meaningfully from the model. Bookmakers and prediction markets both assign Germany 55% and Ecuador roughly 19%, giving the favorite a 20-point advantage. This represents a substantial gap between the model's near-parity reading and the market's stronger conviction in Germany. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is correct.
